home.faq_q1: What is the best Tesla camping mattress?
home.faq_a1: The Snuuzu Model Y is our highest editorial score at 9.4/10. The current version combines comfort foam, an adjustable surface-flattening air layer and an integrated USB-C pump. The score is based on current specifications, value and attributed owner-feedback patterns—not hands-on test nights.
home.faq_q2: Do Tesla mattresses fit all Model Y years?
home.faq_a2: Compatibility varies by product and generation. Current listings explicitly include Juniper for Snuuzu, NovaPads, Havnby FlatCore and TESMAT Horizon, but buyers should still select the exact vehicle variant and confirm the live fit statement before ordering.
home.faq_q3: How much do Tesla camping mattresses cost?
home.faq_a3: The products in our current comparison range from $140 for a TESMAT Solo to €899/$899 for Snuuzu, based on dated price snapshots. Promotions, currency, tax and delivery can change the final cost.
home.faq_q4: Are Tesla-specific mattresses worth it over generic air mattresses?
home.faq_a4: A Tesla-specific mattress can reduce wheel-well gaps and better match the folded-seat shape, while a generic air mattress can be cheaper and more versatile. The better choice depends on verified fit, packed size, support needs and how often you camp.
home.faq_q5: How do you rate Tesla camping mattresses?
home.faq_a5: We check manufacturer specifications, attribute owner-report themes, explain evidence limits and revise pages when products change. We do not claim hands-on testing that was not performed, and manufacturers do not approve our ratings.

about.p: Teslamattress is an independent review site for Tesla camping mattresses. Reviews use current manufacturer specifications, model-specific fit analysis, dated prices and attributed patterns in published owner feedback. We clearly identify seller claims and do not present secondary research as hands-on testing.
about.p_2: Unlike manufacturer-sponsored reviews, we have no obligation to any brand. Our ratings reflect the evidence we can verify, and we update reviews as products evolve.
about.p_3: Every mattress we review goes through the same research protocol:
about.p_4: Our scoring system evaluates five key areas: Comfort, Build Quality, Fit & Finish, Ease of Setup, and Value for Money.
about.p_5: Teslamattress earns money through affiliate partnerships. When you use our discount codes or click our links to purchase a mattress, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.
about.p_6: Important: Affiliate relationships never influence our ratings. We've given low scores to products from brands we partner with, and high scores to brands without affiliate programs. Our credibility depends on honest reviews.
about.p_7: Read our full Affiliate Disclosure for more details.
about.p_8: I'm Fredrik Mastouri, the editor behind Teslamattress. This site started as a research project: Tesla camping had exploded in popularity, but reliable information about which mattresses actually fit which models was scattered across forum threads, spec sheets, and marketing pages.
about.p_9: Collecting, cross-checking, and organizing that information, spec by spec and owner report by owner report, turned into this site.
about.p_10: Today the work follows a steady editorial routine: verifying manufacturer specs, reading owner reviews on Tesla forums and Reddit, tracking prices, and re-checking discount codes before each update.
about.p_11: I use "we" throughout the site because Teslamattress runs as an editorial project rather than a personal blog. The goal stays the same either way: helping you find the right mattress for your Tesla.
about.p_13: Have questions about a specific mattress? Found an error in our reviews? Want to suggest a product for us to cover?
about.p_14: We'd love to hear from you:

review_snuuzu_model_y.meta_title: Snuuzu Model Y Review 2026 — Premium Tesla Mattress
review_snuuzu_model_y.meta_description: Snuuzu Model Y review with current specs, fit, setup, owner-feedback themes and tradeoffs. A 20 cm Tesla mattress with USB-C pump and 3-year warranty.
review_snuuzu_model_y.og_title: Snuuzu Model Y Review 2026 — Premium Tesla Mattress
review_snuuzu_model_y.og_description: Current Snuuzu Model Y specs, fit, setup and tradeoffs, checked against the manufacturer listing and owner feedback.
review_snuuzu_model_y.h1: Snuuzu Model Y Review (2026): Premium Comfort, Current Specs
review_snuuzu_model_y.tldr: Our premium comfort pick for frequent Model Y campers: 20 cm deep, adjustable and equipped with an integrated USB-C pump. The downside is the €899/$899 price.
review_snuuzu_model_y.body_html:
What the current Snuuzu Model Y actually is

Snuuzu has updated this product over time, so older descriptions that call it a pure-foam pad are no longer accurate. The current mattress is a multi-layer sleep system: a removable Tencel top, pressure-relieving comfort foam, an airflow mesh layer, an adjustable air layer that smooths the folded-seat surface, and a protective base with an integrated carry bag. A rechargeable pump is built into the system and charges by USB-C.

Our assessment is based on the current manufacturer specification sheet and recurring themes in published owner feedback. We have not run a hands-on sleep lab, so setup, comfort and durability comments are identified as vendor claims or owner-reported patterns rather than our own measurements.

Current specifications

SpecificationSnuuzu Model Y
Price€899 / $899 for the mattress-only option
Sleeping surface204 × 130 cm
Thickness20 cm for the Model Y version
Package weight10.6 kg
Packed size74 × 32 cm
SetupUnder 2 minutes, according to Snuuzu
PumpIntegrated rechargeable pump, USB-C charging
Warranty3 years
CompatibilityAll Model Y versions, including Juniper, per Snuuzu

Why it ranks highly

The strongest part of the design is that comfort and leveling are treated as one system. The foam layer handles pressure distribution while the shaped air layer fills the dips created by the folded rear seats. That is more considered than placing a rectangular camping pad over the cargo floor and hoping bedding hides the gaps.

The 130 cm width is generous within a Model Y and the 204 cm length is useful for taller sleepers. Snuuzu also says the packed mattress fits in the sub-trunk, which matters on a road trip because the sleeping surface should not consume the cargo area while you are driving.

Strengths

  • Wide, long sleeping surface designed for two adults
  • Foam comfort plus an adjustable leveling air layer
  • Integrated rechargeable USB-C pump
  • Washable Tencel cover and replaceable parts
  • Packs into an attached bag sized for Tesla storage

Tradeoffs

  • €899/$899 is the highest price in our current shortlist
  • 10.6 kg is substantial when moving it in and out of the car
  • The product still relies on an air layer and charged pump
  • Snuuzu says return shipping is normally paid by the customer
  • Used or unsealed mattress returns are restricted for hygiene reasons

Setup and battery reality

Snuuzu lists a setup and pack-away time of under two minutes. The pump is rechargeable and removable, with USB-C charging. The current listing states up to seven complete setup-and-pack cycles on a charge; it also lists about 30 minutes of charging for one inflation and around four hours for a full charge. Those are manufacturer figures, not our stopwatch results.

The practical advantage is fewer loose components. The practical risk is arriving with an empty pump battery. Charge before a trip and keep a USB-C cable in the car. Newer product information also mentions replaceable pumps and other spare parts, which is more useful for long-term ownership than treating the mattress as a sealed disposable product.

What owner feedback adds

Published owner feedback most often highlights the bed-like surface, fast setup and the way the shaped layer reduces the folded-seat dip. The recurring objections are predictable: the purchase price, the packed weight and the effort of rolling a large mattress tightly enough for storage. Those tradeoffs matter more than a single star average, which is why our score remains an editorial assessment rather than copied retailer rating markup.

Who should buy it?

The Snuuzu makes the most sense for a Model Y owner who camps repeatedly, sleeps as a couple, values a larger surface and wants a more complete sleep system than a thin foam pad. It is difficult to justify for one or two nights a year. In that case, compare the lower-priced TESMAT Solo, NovaPads and Havnby FlatCore before paying the premium.

Skip the Snuuzu Model Y if…

  • You need the lightest possible kit.
  • You do not want any inflatable layer or pump dependency.
  • You camp alone and prefer to keep half the cargo area open.
  • You are unsure about fit for another Tesla model; use the model-specific version.

Model fit and warranty

Snuuzu currently states that the Model Y version fits every Model Y generation, including the Juniper refresh. The warranty is three years and covers defects in materials and workmanship. The company also advertises replaceable covers, pumps, charging cables and related parts. Read the current return conditions before opening the mattress because hygiene restrictions can affect eligibility.

Bottom line

This is a premium, feature-rich Model Y mattress rather than the pure-foam product described in some older reviews. Its strongest case is the combination of a broad 204 × 130 cm surface, comfort foam, surface-flattening air layer and compact integrated storage. Its weakest case is price. Our 9.4/10 score reflects category design, specifications and the pattern in owner feedback—not personal test nights.

Evidence and sources

This is a secondary-research review, not a hands-on test. We checked the dimensions, construction, included pump, compatibility and warranty against the current Snuuzu US listing on 22 July 2026. Setup times are manufacturer claims. Owner-feedback themes are used only where they are identified as such; they are not a substitute for controlled durability or comfort testing.

review_snuuzu_model_3.meta_title: Snuuzu Model 3 Review 2026 — Fit, Specs & Verdict
review_snuuzu_model_3.meta_description: Snuuzu Model 3 review with current 2026 specs, Highland fit, setup, comfort layers and tradeoffs. Includes USB-C pump, 18 cm thickness and 3-year warranty.
review_snuuzu_model_3.og_title: Snuuzu Model 3 Review 2026 — Current Specs and Verdict
review_snuuzu_model_3.og_description: Current Snuuzu Model 3 fit, specifications and tradeoffs, checked against the manufacturer listing and owner feedback.
review_snuuzu_model_3.h1: Snuuzu Model 3 Review (2026): Current Fit, Specs and Verdict
review_snuuzu_model_3.tldr: Our premium Model 3 pick, including Highland: an 18 cm foam-and-air system with an integrated USB-C pump. It is comfortable and polished, but expensive and relatively heavy.
review_snuuzu_model_3.body_html:
A Model 3-specific version, not a cut-down generic pad

The current Snuuzu Model 3 uses the same overall architecture as the Model Y product: a removable Tencel cover, comfort foam, airflow mesh, a shaped adjustable air layer and a protective base with integrated storage. The difference that matters is below the sleeper. Snuuzu shapes the leveling layer around the Model 3's folded-seat geometry, so the company recommends using the correct vehicle variant even though the overall bed size is shared.

This review synthesizes the live manufacturer specification sheet and published owner feedback. We have not performed hands-on laboratory testing, and all setup or battery figures below are clearly treated as manufacturer claims.

Current Snuuzu Model 3 specifications

SpecificationSnuuzu Model 3
Price€899 / $899 for the mattress-only option
Sleeping surface204 × 130 cm
Thickness18 cm for the Model 3 version
Package weight10.6 kg
Packed size74 × 32 cm
SetupUnder 2 minutes, according to Snuuzu
PumpIntegrated rechargeable pump, USB-C charging
Warranty3 years
CompatibilityAll Model 3 versions, including Highland, per Snuuzu

Why the Model 3 version matters

A Model 3 and Model Y do not create the same contour when their seats are folded. Snuuzu's own fit guidance says the wrong variant may physically fit but will leave the hip area slightly raised or dipped because the surface-flattening layer is positioned for another car. For a product whose main advantage is correcting that contour, ordering by vehicle is essential.

The 18 cm Model 3 thickness is two centimetres lower than the Model Y version, helping preserve a little more cabin space. The listed sleeping surface remains 204 × 130 cm, making this a genuine two-person option on paper rather than a narrow solo mat.

Strengths

  • Purpose-shaped leveling layer for Model 3 and Highland
  • Long and wide sleeping surface for the vehicle class
  • Comfort foam plus adjustable firmness
  • Integrated rechargeable USB-C pump
  • Washable cover and replaceable components

Tradeoffs

  • €899/$899 is premium pricing
  • 10.6 kg package weight is not especially portable
  • Requires a charged pump and careful pack-away
  • Lower Model 3 roof still limits sitting headroom
  • Return conditions are stricter once a mattress is opened or used

Setup, storage and daily use

Snuuzu claims under two minutes for setup and pack-away. The pump charges over USB-C, and the current specification lists up to seven complete setup-and-pack cycles per full charge. The 74 × 32 cm packed bag is designed to fit in Tesla underfloor storage, though the 10.6 kg weight means compact does not equal light.

The removable Tencel cover is washable at 30°C according to Snuuzu. That is useful in a Model 3, where the mattress sits close to trim, shoes and luggage. Let the interior and mattress dry before packing after damp nights; no fabric or foam system benefits from being sealed away wet.

Who should buy it?

Choose the Model 3 Snuuzu if you camp often, want the widest premium surface available for the sedan and value a system designed around Highland as well as earlier cars. If price matters more than maximum comfort, the TESMAT Horizon Model 3 and TESMAT Solo Model 3 deserve comparison.

Skip this mattress if…

  • You only expect one or two camping nights per year.
  • You want a lightweight solo mat.
  • You prefer a product without an inflatable layer.
  • You own a Model Y; use the version shaped for that vehicle.

Warranty and return details

The current warranty is three years for defects in materials and workmanship. Snuuzu lists replaceable covers, pumps and charging accessories, which improves repairability. Returns are not advertised as free, and opened or used mattress returns may be restricted for hygiene reasons, so check the policy for your regional storefront before ordering.

Bottom line

The Snuuzu Model 3 is one of the few premium systems built around the sedan's actual folded-seat geometry. Its current specifications are materially different from older pure-foam descriptions: this is a foam-and-air system with an integrated pump, adjustable firmness and compact storage. Our 9.3/10 score is editorial and based on current specifications plus owner-feedback patterns, not invented test nights.

Evidence and sources

This is a secondary-research review, not a hands-on test. We checked the dimensions, construction, included pump, Highland compatibility and warranty against the current Snuuzu US listing on 22 July 2026. Setup times are manufacturer claims, and comfort remains subjective.

review_havnby_autolevel.meta_title: Havnby FlatCore Foam Mattress Review 2026 — Model Y
review_havnby_autolevel.meta_description: Havnby FlatCore Hybrid review with current specs: $599, 193 × 132 cm, 5.08 kg, built-in 12V pump, 80-second inflation and 3-year warranty.
review_havnby_autolevel.og_title: Havnby FlatCore Foam Mattress Review — Current Specs
review_havnby_autolevel.og_description: Current Havnby FlatCore Model Y specifications, fit, setup and tradeoffs, checked against the live manufacturer listing.
review_havnby_autolevel.h1: Havnby FlatCore Foam Mattress Review (2026): Model Y Leveling Support
review_havnby_autolevel.tldr: Our best mid-price leveling pick: a 19 cm contoured Model Y mattress with a built-in 12V pump. At $599 it undercuts Snuuzu, but the raised hatch end reduces headroom.
review_havnby_autolevel.body_html:
Autolevel has become FlatCore

Havnby now sells the former Autolevel product as the FlatCore Hybrid Support Mattress for Tesla Model Y. The central idea remains the same: vary the mattress depth to counter the slope of the folded rear seats, add foam over the air structure, and integrate a 12V pump so no loose inflator is required.

Our review is based on Havnby's current specification table and recurring themes in the brand's published customer feedback. We have not performed hands-on laboratory testing. Inflation times, dimensions and load capacity below are manufacturer figures.

Current FlatCore specifications

SpecificationHavnby FlatCore Hybrid
Price$599
FitTesla Model Y; separate 2020–2024 and 2025–2026 options
Size193 × 132 cm
Thickness19 cm thickest / 7.9 cm thinnest
Weight5.08 kg
Packed size67 × 43 cm
Inflation / deflation80 seconds / 90 seconds, vendor claim
PumpBuilt-in 12V ABS pump
Maximum load300 kg, vendor claim
Trial / warranty60-day sleep trial / 3-year warranty

How the leveling design works

The rear seats of a Model Y do not form a perfectly level bedroom floor. FlatCore is thicker where more support is needed and thinner where the cargo floor is already higher. Havnby describes this as correcting seat angles up to 12 degrees. That figure describes the product's intended correction range, not a measurement we made inside a vehicle.

The built-in pump fills the mattress in a claimed 80 seconds. It is powered from a 12V connection rather than an internal rechargeable battery, which trades charging management for a cable in the cabin. Foam above the air structure aims to reduce the bouncy feel associated with a basic air bed.

Strengths

  • Shaped specifically to reduce the Model Y cargo slope
  • 19 cm maximum depth gives substantial cushioning
  • Built-in pump and included storage bag
  • Light 5.08 kg listed weight for the size
  • Separate selections for pre-refresh and 2025–2026 Model Y

Tradeoffs

  • $599 costs more than most conventional air-foam pads
  • The thick hatch end reduces sitting headroom
  • Requires access to the car's 12V power connection
  • Some published owners find the cover slippery
  • Three-year warranty is shorter than older five-year descriptions online

What owner feedback contributes

Positive owner reports concentrate on the fitted shape, the reduction in rear-seat slope and the supported head end. Critical reports focus on the height near the hatch, movement on the smooth cover and the amount of room left above the mattress. This is a product where vehicle fit and sleeper preference matter: deeper is useful for pressure relief but costs cabin volume.

The current product includes the mattress, storage bag, user manual and two eye masks. Havnby advertises a 60-day sleep trial, but shoppers should read the current return conditions and keep packaging until fit is confirmed.

Who should buy the FlatCore?

FlatCore suits Model Y owners who dislike the cargo-floor slope, want a wide couples mattress and prefer a built-in 12V pump over carrying a separate inflator. It is especially relevant for side sleepers who value depth. Taller people who need maximum sitting headroom should compare thinner options before ordering.

Skip the FlatCore if…

  • You want the lowest mattress or maximum cabin headroom.
  • You need a mattress for Model 3, Model S or Model X.
  • You prefer a USB-C rechargeable pump.
  • You camp solo and want to reserve half the cargo floor for equipment.

Bottom line

The renamed FlatCore remains a distinctive Model Y mattress because its geometry addresses the folded-seat slope rather than merely covering it. The current product is smaller and much lighter than some older third-party specifications suggest, and the warranty is now three years. Our 8.6/10 score reflects the design, verified specification set and owner-feedback pattern—not personal test nights.

Evidence and sources

This is a secondary-research review, not a hands-on test. Product dimensions, package contents, vehicle variants, pump type, trial and warranty were checked against the current Havnby FlatCore listing on 23 July 2026. review_havnby_foam.og_description: An archival look at the discontinued Havnby Foam mattress and the current FlatCore and CloudCore alternatives.
review_havnby_foam.h1: Havnby Foam Review (2026): Discontinued Product, Current Alternatives
review_havnby_foam.tldr: Havnby no longer sells this foam mattress, so we keep the review only as a historical reference. Compare the current FlatCore and CloudCore ranges before buying.
review_havnby_foam.body_html:
This product is no longer for sale

Havnby has removed the Foam Model Y/3 mattress from its current store. That changes how this page should be read: it is an archive for owners and second-hand shoppers, not a recommendation to buy from an unavailable listing.

We cannot verify the discontinued product's old dimensions, package contents, price or warranty against a live manufacturer specification table. Older copies of this review repeated conflicting measurements and should not be treated as current product facts. For a new purchase, use Havnby's live FlatCore or CloudCore listings instead.

What replaced it?

Current optionUse caseWhat to check
FlatCore HybridFull-width Model Y sleep surface with leveling supportSelect the correct Model Y year range
FlatCore SoloOne sleeper with cargo space left beside the bedSolo width and seating configuration
CloudCoreLower-profile current Havnby alternativeVehicle variant, current dimensions and package contents

If you find a used Havnby Foam

Ask for the exact model label, original instructions and clear photographs of valves, seams, foam surface and packed condition. Confirm whether the seller's unit was made for Model Y or Model 3 rather than assuming one shape fits both. Because the product is discontinued, ask Havnby whether replacement parts or warranty support remain available for that serial number before paying.

A used unit may make sense if…

  • The seller can document the exact vehicle fit
  • You can inspect the condition before purchase
  • The price reflects discontinued support
  • You only need an occasional-use mattress

Skip it if…

  • The model, dimensions or valve condition are unclear
  • You need a current trial or warranty
  • The mattress has been stored damp or compressed for a long period
  • A current fitted product is available near the same total price

Bottom line

The Havnby Foam page should no longer compete as if the product were current. Its useful SEO role is answering owners who encounter the old name and directing them to verifiable alternatives. Our historical 7.6/10 score remains visible for continuity, but it is not a current product endorsement and is not based on invented test nights.

Evidence and sources

This archive page is based on Havnby's current range and the absence of the old Foam model from the manufacturer's live storefront, checked 22 July 2026. It is not a hands-on review and should not be read as proof that every used unit has the same specification or support status.

review_havnby_cloudcore.meta_title: Havnby CloudCore Review 2026 — Tesla S/3/X/Y Mattress
review_havnby_cloudcore.meta_description: Havnby CloudCore review: $399, 193 × 132 × 11.5 cm, 3.95 kg, built-in pump, Model S/3/X/Y fit, 60-day trial and 3-year warranty.
review_havnby_cloudcore.og_title: Havnby CloudCore Foam Mattress Review — Current Specs
review_havnby_cloudcore.og_description: A current, evidence-led look at Havnby's $399 CloudCore Foam Mattress for Tesla Model S, 3, X and Y.
review_havnby_cloudcore.h1: Havnby CloudCore Review (2026): One Mattress for Model S, 3, X and Y
review_havnby_cloudcore.tldr: Havnby's most versatile current mattress fits listed Model S, 3, X and Y variants. At $399, its 11.5 cm foam-and-air construction is thinner than FlatCore and easier to move, but shoppers must select the exact car and year.
review_havnby_cloudcore.body_html:
A current cross-model Havnby mattress

CloudCore is one of the three current Havnby Tesla Model Y mattress options, alongside the full-width FlatCore Foam Mattress and the FlatCore Solo Edition. Unlike those Model Y-specific products, Havnby currently offers CloudCore variants for Tesla Model S, Model 3, Model X and Model Y.

This review is based on Havnby's live product listing and published specification table. We have not conducted hands-on sleep testing. Dimensions, weight, setup times, load capacity, trial and warranty below are manufacturer figures checked on 23 July 2026.

Current CloudCore specifications

SpecificationHavnby CloudCore
Observed list price$399
Listed compatibilityTesla Model S, 3, X and Y; choose model and year
Sleeping size193 × 132 × 11.5 cm
Weight3.95 kg
Packed size72 × 40 × 40 cm
Inflation / deflation70 seconds / 85 seconds, vendor claim
PumpBuilt-in pump
Maximum load300 kg, vendor claim
Trial / warranty60-day sleep trial / 3-year warranty

How CloudCore differs from FlatCore

CloudCore is the range's cross-model choice. Its published 11.5 cm depth is lower than the shaped FlatCore's 19 cm maximum, while its 3.95 kg listed weight is also lower than FlatCore's 5.08 kg. That makes CloudCore more attractive when portability and broader vehicle selection matter more than the deepest Model Y-specific leveling profile.

The tradeoff is that a single published 193 × 132 cm surface does not prove identical edge fit in every Tesla cabin. Havnby asks shoppers to select a vehicle and model year, so the exact option in the cart matters. Confirm the variant rather than assuming a Model Y unit will automatically suit a Model 3, S or X.

Strengths

  • Current variants listed for Model S, 3, X and Y
  • Lower $399 observed price than the full-width FlatCore
  • Light 3.95 kg published weight
  • Built-in pump and included storage bag
  • 60-day trial and three-year warranty advertised by Havnby

Tradeoffs

  • Vehicle and model-year selection must be checked carefully
  • Thinner than the deepest part of FlatCore
  • Inflation, deflation and load figures are seller claims
  • Foam-and-air construction still depends on valves and an air chamber
  • Live price, stock and return conditions can change

Setup and storage

Havnby lists 70 seconds to inflate and 85 seconds to deflate. The product is supplied with the CloudCore mattress, storage bag, user manual and two eye masks. The 72 × 40 × 40 cm packed dimensions are larger than a backpacking pad, so compare them with the space you expect to keep free while driving.

The stated 300 kg capacity describes the seller's maximum load claim, not our independent measurement. Couples should also compare usable width, combined shoulder room and preferred firmness instead of treating a capacity number as proof of comfort.

Who should choose CloudCore?

CloudCore is the clearest Havnby option for a Tesla owner who wants one current product family covering several Tesla models, or a Model Y owner who wants a lower price and lower profile than FlatCore. Choose FlatCore when Model Y-specific slope correction and greater maximum depth matter more. Choose the Solo Edition when one sleeper wants a dedicated cargo lane beside the bed.

Skip CloudCore if…

  • You want the deepest Model Y-specific leveling support.
  • You need a one-person mattress that leaves half the cargo floor open.
  • You are unwilling to verify the exact vehicle and year variant.
  • You want a pure-foam mattress with no inflatable chamber.

Bottom line

CloudCore fills a genuine gap in Havnby's current lineup: it is the $399, lighter cross-model alternative, while FlatCore focuses on deeper Model Y support and FlatCore Solo focuses on one-person storage efficiency. Our 8.0/10 score is an editorial assessment of current specifications, fit breadth, value and evidence limits—not a claim of personal test nights.

Evidence and sources

We checked the price, vehicle variants, dimensions, weight, packed size, included items, setup claims, trial and warranty against the current Havnby CloudCore listing on 23 July 2026. Manufacturer claims are identified as such; shoppers should confirm the live variant, price and terms before ordering.

review_havnby_solo.meta_title: Havnby FlatCore Foam Mattress Solo Review 2026
review_havnby_solo.meta_description: Havnby FlatCore Solo review: $399, 193 × 80 cm, 17 cm thick, 3.29 kg, built-in 12V pump, 50-second inflation and 3-year warranty.
review_havnby_solo.og_title: Havnby FlatCore Foam Mattress — Solo Edition Review
review_havnby_solo.og_description: A current look at Havnby’s solo-width Model Y mattress, with verified dimensions, setup figures and practical tradeoffs.
review_havnby_solo.h1: Havnby FlatCore Foam Mattress — Solo Edition Review (2026)
review_havnby_solo.product_name: Havnby FlatCore Foam Mattress — Solo Edition
review_havnby_solo.tldr: Our best solo-specific pick: a 17 cm Model Y mattress that leaves half the cargo area open for gear. It costs $399 and sets up quickly, but it is strictly for one sleeper.
review_havnby_solo.body_html:
A mattress for one side of the Model Y

The FlatCore Solo uses the same basic leveling concept as the full-width Havnby mattress but narrows the sleeping area to 80 cm. The other side of the Model Y remains available for a cooler, bags, a dog or access to one rear seat. That simple layout is the product's reason to exist.

We assessed the current manufacturer specification table and published owner feedback. We have not conducted hands-on sleep testing. Setup times and measurements below are Havnby's current published figures.

Current FlatCore Solo specifications

SpecificationHavnby FlatCore Solo
Price$399
FitTesla Model Y; separate 2020–2024 and 2025–2026 options
Size193 × 80 × 17 cm
Weight3.29 kg
Packed size72 × 34 cm
Inflation / deflation50 seconds / 60 seconds, vendor claim
PumpBuilt-in 12V ABS pump
Trial / warranty60-day sleep trial / 3-year warranty

The advantage: a real storage lane

A full-width Model Y mattress forces nearly all loose equipment into the front seats or footwells at bedtime. The Solo leaves a parallel cargo lane open. For one-person road trips, that often matters more than extra mattress width because water, shoes and a jacket remain reachable without opening the hatch.

At 80 cm wide, the bed is broader than many backpacking pads but is still strictly a one-person product. The shaped thickness helps reduce the folded-seat slope, while the foam-and-air construction provides more depth than a thin roll-up pad.

Strengths

  • Leaves approximately half the cargo area open
  • Low 3.29 kg listed weight
  • Fast 50-second vendor-claimed inflation
  • Built-in pump and included fitted sheet
  • Model Y-specific leveling shape

Tradeoffs

  • Not suitable for two sleepers
  • $399 is expensive for a narrow mattress
  • Requires a 12V connection
  • Published feedback suggests seven-seat layouts may need extra adjustment
  • Packing tightly into the bag can take practice

Fit nuances worth knowing

Havnby sells separate Model Y year selections, so choose the exact vehicle range at checkout. One published seven-seat Model Y owner reported adding a small support underneath to achieve the level feel they wanted. That does not prove every seven-seater behaves the same way, but it is a useful reminder that seat configuration and preferred firmness affect the result.

The product includes the Solo mattress, storage bag, fitted sheet, user manual and eye masks. Havnby advertises a 60-day sleep trial and three-year warranty. Check the live policy before purchase and preserve the packaging while evaluating fit.

Who should buy the Solo?

Buy it if you usually travel alone and value accessible storage as much as sleep comfort. Choose the full-width FlatCore if a partner may join you, or compare the TESMAT Solo if price and a simpler foam format matter more than leveling depth.

Skip the FlatCore Solo if…

  • You need room for two people.
  • You want a mattress for any Tesla other than Model Y.
  • You dislike sleeping on a relatively narrow surface.
  • You want the least expensive occasional-use solution.

Bottom line

FlatCore Solo solves a specific problem well: one sleeper needs a shaped Model Y bed without giving up the entire cargo area. The verified current size is 193 × 80 × 17 cm and the warranty is three years, correcting older figures still repeated elsewhere online. Our 8.2/10 rating is an editorial score based on specifications and owner-feedback themes, not a claim of personal test nights.

Evidence and sources

This is a secondary-research review, not a hands-on test. Dimensions, included items, vehicle selections, warranty and trial were checked against Havnby's current storefront on 23 July 2026. Setup times are manufacturer claims. The seven-seat fit observation is an individual owner report and may not generalize to every car.

review_tesery_novapads.meta_title: NovaPads Air-Foam Pro Review 2026 — Specs & Value
review_tesery_novapads.meta_description: NovaPads Air-Foam Mattress Pro review with regional price context, dimensions, 4.5-inch air-foam construction, fit options and evidence limits.
review_tesery_novapads.og_title: NovaPads Air-Foam Pro Review: Current Specs and Tradeoffs
review_tesery_novapads.og_description: A source-checked look at NovaPads Air-Foam Mattress Pro specifications, regional pricing and Tesla fit.
review_tesery_novapads.h1: NovaPads Air-Foam Pro Review (2026): Current Specs and Tradeoffs
review_tesery_novapads.tldr: A 4.5-inch air-foam option with a built-in 12V pump and Model Y, Model Y Juniper and Model X variants. The Norway-localized USD storefront showed $239, but regional pricing varies and long-term comfort is not independently tested here.
review_tesery_novapads.body_html:
The NovaPads Air-Foam Mattress Pro sits in the useful middle ground between a basic foam pad and the deepest premium Tesla beds. The storefront localized to Norway (USD) showed $239 with Model Y, Model Y Juniper and Model X variants. This review checks what the listing actually supports, where the value case is strong and what still needs buyer judgment.

Editorial verdict: NovaPads is a credible value candidate for buyers who want a fitted air-foam design without paying premium-mattress prices. Our 8.8/10 score is a specification-and-value assessment, not a claim that we slept on the product.

Current NovaPads Air-Foam Pro specifications

ItemCurrent listing
Observed price$239 in the Norway-localized USD storefront on 23 July 2026; live price can change
Construction4.5-inch air-foam design with high-density foam
Published size193 × 132 × 11 cm (76 × 52 × 4.5 in)
Published weight7 kg (15 lb)
Vehicle choicesModel Y Juniper, Model Y and Model X
InflationBuilt-in 6000Pa pump, 12V supply; seller claims 50-second setup and 60-second storage
Cover and materialRemovable sheet and TPU air structure according to the listing
StorageMarketed to fit a compatible vehicle's frunk or sub-trunk when packed
Returns and warranty30-day returns and a one-year storefront warranty; check live eligibility before opening or inflating

What the design is trying to solve

A pure air mattress packs small but can feel unstable when two sleepers move. A pure foam mattress is simple but takes more cargo space. NovaPads combines foam with an inflatable structure, aiming for a more supported surface while remaining compact enough for vehicle storage. The 4.5-inch profile is deeper than a thin camping mat, but depth alone does not prove pressure relief or cold-weather insulation.

The listing also emphasizes a rear-slope-leveling shape. That is relevant in a Model Y because folded rear seats do not create a perfectly flat bedroom. Fit depends on selecting the correct vehicle variant, so confirm the car generation shown in the selector instead of relying on the product family name.

Strengths on paper

  • Lower observed price than the premium systems in our comparison
  • Foam-and-air construction rather than a basic air-only bed
  • Built-in pump reduces the number of loose accessories
  • Current variants include Model Y Juniper

Important limits

  • No independent long-duration test was performed for this review
  • One-year warranty is shorter than several premium competitors
  • Seller setup demonstrations are not independent timing tests
  • Model 3 is not listed among the current Pro variants

Who should shortlist it?

Shortlist the Air-Foam Pro if you camp occasionally or moderately often, want a fitted Tesla shape and care more about value and compact storage than maximum mattress depth. Couples and side sleepers should compare the measurements and return terms with the deeper Havnby FlatCore and Snuuzu Model Y. Solo travelers who want a simpler, narrower pad can compare the TESMAT Solo Model Y.

Skip or pause if…

  • You need a verified Model 3 fit.
  • You require independently measured cold-weather performance.
  • You prefer a mattress without valves, an air chamber or a pump.
  • The live return and warranty conditions do not cover your intended use.

Price and buying checks

The $239 Norway-store figure is a regional snapshot, not a permanent promise. Check the selected vehicle variant, shipping destination, tax, return conditions and final checkout total. Our NovaPads partner-code page records the last code check separately; a referral link by itself does not prove a discount is active.

Evidence and sources

Specifications and the Norway-localized price were checked against the current NovaPads storefront on 23 July 2026. This is a secondary-research review: we did not run a hands-on sleep, noise, temperature or durability test. Claims about leveling, storage speed and setup come from the seller unless explicitly described otherwise.

It is considerably less expensive than premium alternatives, but shoppers should distinguish the manufacturer specifications from independent test evidence.

review_novapads_auto_leveling.tldr: A new Tesla-shaped air mattress intended to counter the folded-seat slope, with Model Y, Model Y Juniper and Model X variants. The Norway-localized USD storefront showed $369; regional pricing varies, and several detailed specifications remain unpublished.
review_novapads_auto_leveling.body_html:
NovaPads added its Auto-Leveling Camping Mattress in July 2026. The storefront localized to Norway (USD) showed $369, reduced from a displayed $399 comparison price, with selectable versions for Model Y, Model Y Juniper and Model X. It targets one of the main Tesla camping problems: the cargo surface slopes after the rear seats are folded.

Editorial verdict: The Auto-Leveling model is a promising new alternative between NovaPads' $239 Air-Foam Pro and deeper premium systems. Our provisional 8.1/10 score reflects its price position, vehicle-specific variants and useful design purpose. It does not imply hands-on testing, and the incomplete specification sheet keeps it below our more fully documented picks.

Current published facts

ItemCurrent seller listing
Observed price$369 in the Norway-localized USD storefront on 23 July 2026; $399 comparison price displayed
AvailabilityListed as available when checked
Vehicle choicesModel Y Juniper, Model Y and Model X
Stated purposeAuto-leveling support intended to reduce the folded-seat slope
Published dimensionsNot provided on the checked product page
Pump and powerA built-in air pump is included; power source and performance are not specified on the checked page
Product-specific warrantyNot repeated on the checked product page; confirm the live terms

Why the leveling concept matters

A folded Tesla rear seat creates a sleeping platform, but not a perfectly level one. A mattress designed to compensate for that geometry can reduce the need for separate wedges or improvised supports. NovaPads positions this model around that problem. The current listing does not explain the internal chamber layout or publish a correction angle, so we cannot independently compare its leveling range with Havnby's shaped FlatCore specification.

The seller provides separate Model Y, Juniper and Model X choices. That is useful because a product family name alone does not prove fit. Buyers should still confirm the exact vehicle and year selection in the cart and ask for the current dimensions if their height or cargo layout is close to the limit.

What looks useful

  • Targets the real folded-seat slope rather than acting as a generic rectangle
  • Current choices include Model Y Juniper and Model X
  • $369 observed price sits below the deeper premium systems we track
  • Available now on the checked storefront rather than shown as a pre-order

What remains unverified

  • No dimensions or packed size published on the checked page
  • No pump type, power source or setup time specified
  • No independent comfort, noise or durability test
  • Product-specific return and warranty wording needs confirmation before purchase

Auto-Leveling versus Air-Foam Pro

The $239 NovaPads Air-Foam Pro has a fuller current specification set, including a 4.5-inch air-foam description and built-in pump. The newer Auto-Leveling listing emphasizes slope correction but costs $130 more at the observed prices. Without published dimensions and pump details, the premium cannot yet be justified from specifications alone.

Choose the Air-Foam Pro when the lower price and documented construction matter most. Keep the Auto-Leveling model on the shortlist when slope correction is the priority and NovaPads confirms the missing details for your exact vehicle.

Who should consider it?

Consider it if you own one of the listed Tesla models, dislike the cargo-floor slope and are comfortable asking the seller for exact dimensions, pump details and return terms before ordering. If you want a more fully documented shaped system, compare the Havnby FlatCore. If price matters most, compare the Air-Foam Pro and the under-$300 shortlist.

Wait before ordering if…

  • You need a published bed length or packed size before making the decision.
  • You require a confirmed pump type or an exact inflation time.
  • You want established long-term owner evidence for this specific model.
  • The seller cannot confirm return eligibility after opening or inflating it.

Price, code and source check

The Norway-localized NovaPads storefront showed $369 with a $399 comparison price when checked on 23 July 2026. Prices, availability and sale framing can change. Our NovaPads code page records code AWD separately; confirm acceptance and the final amount in the live cart.

This review is based on the current NovaPads storefront and its public Shopify product data. We have not run a hands-on sleep, fit, inflation or durability test. Where the seller has not published a specification, we leave it unknown rather than infer it from another NovaPads model.

review_tesmat_luxe_y.h1: TESMAT Horizon Model Y Review (2026): Pre-Order Facts and Tradeoffs
review_tesmat_luxe_y.tldr: A six-inch hybrid kit with USB-C pump, fitted sheet and carry case, listed at a $339 sale price. It was still a pre-order for August 2026 when checked, so long-term evidence for the current version is limited.
review_tesmat_luxe_y.body_html:
TESMAT now sells the product previously called Luxe as the Horizon Model Y. The current listing describes a six-inch hybrid self-inflating mattress and, on 22 July 2026, marked it as a pre-order expected to ship in August 2026. That timing matters: older Luxe reviews and long-term owner claims cannot automatically be transferred to this revised product.

Editorial verdict: The Horizon Model Y is a promising all-in-one kit at the observed $339 sale price, but it should be judged as a current pre-order product. Our score reflects the specification, kit completeness and price position—not personal test nights or proven long-term durability.

Current Horizon Model Y facts

ItemCurrent listing
Status checked 22 July 2026Pre-order; seller states August 2026 shipping
Observed price$339 sale price; $489 comparison price shown by TESMAT
Depth6 inches
ConstructionSelf-inflating air structure, dual-foam support and a 3D internal grid
IncludedWireless USB-C pump, fitted microfiber sheet and carry case
Inflation choicesSelf-inflation, included pump or an air-filled carry-case method
CompatibilityAll Model Y generations, including Juniper, according to TESMAT

Why the new version is different

The six-inch specification makes the current Horizon materially different from the thinner dimensions still attached to the old Luxe name around the web. TESMAT says the internal design combines air with two foam layers and a 3D grid. Those details support the expectation of more cushioning than a thin roll-up pad, but they do not establish a universal comfort result.

The included sheet, pump and carry case make this a more complete first-purchase package. TESMAT advertises pump inflation in under a minute and self-inflation in about 20 minutes. Treat both as manufacturer claims until independently timed in repeatable conditions.

Strong points

  • Six-inch hybrid construction at a mid-range observed price
  • Pump, fitted sheet and carry case included
  • Current compatibility includes Juniper
  • Three inflation methods provide useful redundancy

Reasons for caution

  • It was still a pre-order when this page was checked
  • Current long-term owner evidence is necessarily limited
  • Six inches uses more cabin height than a slim foam pad
  • Sale and shipping dates can change

Who should consider it?

The Horizon Model Y suits a buyer who wants a complete kit and more depth than the TESMAT Solo Model Y. Frequent campers should also compare its live warranty, packed size and return rules with our Snuuzu Model Y review. Buyers who need a mattress immediately should confirm the dispatch date before paying.

Wait or choose another option if…

  • You cannot accept a pre-order delivery window.
  • You want extensive owner evidence for the current version.
  • You prioritize the lowest price or smallest packed pad.
  • You have not confirmed the exact vehicle selection at checkout.

Evidence and sources

Price, availability, construction, included items, compatibility and setup claims were checked against the official TESMAT Horizon Model Y listing on 22 July 2026. This is secondary research, not a hands-on test. Availability and setup figures are seller claims and should be reconfirmed on the live product page.
review_tesmat_luxe_y.review_body_summary: The current Horizon is deeper and materially different from the older Luxe specification. It is a well-equipped mid-price candidate, but its pre-order status and limited current-version evidence must be part of the buying decision.
review_tesmat_luxe_y.positive_notes: Six-inch hybrid design, wireless USB-C pump, fitted sheet, carry case and current Juniper compatibility.
review_tesmat_luxe_y.negative_notes: Pre-order status when checked, limited current-version owner evidence and more cabin-height use than a thin pad.

review_tesmat_luxe_3.meta_title: TESMAT Horizon Model 3 Review 2026 — Pre-Order Facts
review_tesmat_luxe_3.meta_description: TESMAT Horizon Model 3 review with current $339 pre-order price, six-inch hybrid design, Highland fit, included kit and limitations.
review_tesmat_luxe_3.og_title: TESMAT Horizon Model 3 Review: Current Pre-Order Facts
review_tesmat_luxe_3.og_description: A source-checked review of the revised six-inch Horizon Model 3 sleep kit.
review_tesmat_luxe_3.h1: TESMAT Horizon Model 3 Review (2026): Current Specs and Caveats
review_tesmat_luxe_3.tldr: A six-inch Model 3 hybrid kit with USB-C pump, fitted sheet and carry case, listed at a $339 sale price. It was a pre-order for August 2026 when checked, so current-version owner evidence is limited.
review_tesmat_luxe_3.body_html:
TESMAT now sells the product previously called Luxe as the Horizon Model 3. The current listing describes a six-inch hybrid self-inflating mattress and, on 22 July 2026, marked it as a pre-order expected to ship in August 2026. That timing matters: older Luxe reviews and long-term owner claims cannot automatically be transferred to this revised product.

Editorial verdict: The Horizon Model 3 is a promising all-in-one kit at the observed $339 sale price, but it should be judged as a current pre-order product. Our score reflects the specification, kit completeness and price position—not personal test nights or proven long-term durability.

Current Horizon Model 3 facts

ItemCurrent listing
Status checked 22 July 2026Pre-order; seller states August 2026 shipping
Observed price$339 sale price; $489 comparison price shown by TESMAT
Depth6 inches
ConstructionSelf-inflating air structure, dual-foam support and a 3D internal grid
IncludedWireless USB-C pump, fitted microfiber sheet and carry case
Inflation choicesSelf-inflation, included pump or an air-filled carry-case method
CompatibilityAll Model 3 generations, including Highland, according to TESMAT

Why the new version is different

The six-inch specification makes the current Horizon materially different from the thinner dimensions still attached to the old Luxe name around the web. TESMAT says the internal design combines air with two foam layers and a 3D grid. Those details support the expectation of more cushioning than a thin roll-up pad, but they do not establish a universal comfort result.

The included sheet, pump and carry case make this a more complete first-purchase package. TESMAT advertises pump inflation in under a minute and self-inflation in about 20 minutes. Treat both as manufacturer claims until independently timed in repeatable conditions.

Strong points

  • Six-inch hybrid construction at a mid-range observed price
  • Pump, fitted sheet and carry case included
  • Current compatibility includes Highland
  • Three inflation methods provide useful redundancy

Reasons for caution

  • It was still a pre-order when this page was checked
  • Current long-term owner evidence is necessarily limited
  • Six inches uses more cabin height than a slim foam pad
  • Sale and shipping dates can change

Who should consider it?

The Horizon Model 3 suits a buyer who wants a complete kit and more depth than the TESMAT Solo Model 3. Frequent campers should also compare its live warranty, packed size and return rules with our Snuuzu Model 3 review. Buyers who need a mattress immediately should confirm the dispatch date before paying.

Wait or choose another option if…

  • You cannot accept a pre-order delivery window.
  • You want extensive owner evidence for the current version.
  • You prioritize the lowest price or smallest packed pad.
  • You have not confirmed the exact vehicle selection at checkout.

Evidence and sources

Price, availability, construction, included items, compatibility and setup claims were checked against the official TESMAT Horizon Model 3 listing on 22 July 2026. This is secondary research, not a hands-on test. Availability and setup figures are seller claims and should be reconfirmed on the live product page.

review_tesmat_luxe_3.positive_notes: Six-inch hybrid kit with USB-C pump, fitted sheet, carry case and Highland compatibility.
review_tesmat_luxe_3.negative_notes: Pre-order delivery window, limited current-version owner evidence and no independently verified setup time.

review_tesmat_luxe_3.cta_text: The Model 3 complete kit now sells as TESMAT Horizon for $339 direct from TESMAT.

review_tesmat_solo_y.meta_title: TESMAT Solo Model Y Review 2026 — Current $140 Pad
review_tesmat_solo_y.meta_description: TESMAT Solo Model Y review covering the current $140 price, three-inch gel foam, six-inch pillow, solo format and key tradeoffs.
review_tesmat_solo_y.og_title: TESMAT Solo Model Y Review: Simple One-Person Foam Pad
review_tesmat_solo_y.og_description: Current specifications, best use cases and limits of the TESMAT Solo Model Y.
review_tesmat_solo_y.h1: TESMAT Solo Model Y Review (2026): Simple, Narrow and Affordable
review_tesmat_solo_y.tldr: A $140 one-person Model Y pad with three-inch gel memory foam, an integrated six-inch pillow and no pump. It preserves cargo space, but couples and pressure-sensitive side sleepers should compare deeper options.
review_tesmat_solo_y.body_html:
The TESMAT Solo Model Y is a narrow one-person foam mattress for owners who want to keep the rest of the car available for luggage or a passenger. The current listing showed $140 on 22 July 2026, with three inches of gel memory foam, a six-inch integrated pillow and a carry case.

Editorial verdict: This is a simple, lower-cost solo option rather than a full-width replacement bed. Our score reflects price, current specifications and the restricted one-person use case—not hands-on sleep or durability testing.

Current TESMAT Solo Model Y specifications

ItemCurrent listing
Observed price$140 on 22 July 2026; live promotions can change
Mattress3-inch gel memory foam
PillowIntegrated 6-inch pillow section
FormatOne-person mattress with room left beside it
IncludedMattress and carry case
StorageAdvertised for compatible frunk or sub-trunk storage when packed

What the Solo format gets right

The most useful feature is not a technical material claim; it is the narrow footprint. A solo traveler can preserve part of the cargo area for equipment and does not have to inflate an air chamber. The integrated pillow also reduces the number of pieces to pack, although pillow height and preference are personal.

Three inches of foam may be enough for some back sleepers and occasional trips, but the specification does not guarantee pressure relief for every body type. Side sleepers and frequent campers should compare a deeper option such as the premium Model Y mattress. Couples need a full-width design rather than two assumptions about how separate pads will meet.

Strong points

  • Low observed entry price among fitted Tesla products
  • No pump, power cable or air valve required
  • Leaves usable space beside a solo sleeper
  • Integrated pillow and carry case simplify packing

Tradeoffs

  • Designed for one sleeper only
  • Three-inch depth is modest for pressure-sensitive sleepers
  • No adjustable firmness
  • Comfort and packed-size claims are not independently tested here

Who should buy it?

Consider the Solo if your priority is a simple fitted pad for occasional one-person trips. Compare the other TESMAT Solo vehicle version only to understand the format—not as an interchangeable fit. Confirm the selected car model, current return policy and delivery date before ordering.

Skip this format if…

  • A second person may regularly join you.
  • You know you need deeper side-sleeper cushioning.
  • You want an adjustable or self-leveling air layer.
  • The listing does not clearly confirm your exact vehicle generation.

Evidence and sources

Price, foam depth, pillow, included case and storage claims were checked against the official TESMAT Solo Model Y listing on 22 July 2026. This is a secondary-research review, not a hands-on test. We found no controlled evidence supporting precise comfort, temperature or lifetime predictions, so none are presented as facts.
review_tesmat_solo_y.review_body_summary: TESMAT Solo Model Y is a focused one-person foam pad. Its simplicity and price are appealing for occasional trips, while its narrow format and modest depth define the compromise.
review_tesmat_solo_y.positive_notes: Low $140 observed price, no pump dependency, integrated pillow and useful space beside a solo sleeper.
review_tesmat_solo_y.negative_notes: One-person format, modest three-inch depth and no adjustable firmness or leveling air layer.
review_tesmat_solo_y.cta_text: Sold direct by TESMAT for $140; check the current checkout price before ordering.

vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p: Two fitted foam-and-air systems with different prices, pump designs and published dimensions.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_2: Choose from the trade-offs, not a blanket winner. Snuuzu publishes a deeper 20 cm system with an integrated pump; TESMAT Horizon costs less and bundles a separate wireless pump and fitted sheet. Both have Model Y and Model 3 versions, so verify the exact vehicle variant and current dispatch date.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_3: Prices and seller specifications checked 22 July 2026. Horizon was listed as a pre-order shipping in August 2026; availability can change.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_4: Snuuzu lists a washable Tencel cover, comfort foam, an airflow layer and an adjustable surface-flattening air layer. The current Model Y listing gives a 20 cm height, 204 × 130 cm sleeping surface and 74 × 32 cm packed size. These are seller specifications, not results from our own sleep test.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_5: TESMAT Horizon lists a 6-inch hybrid of dual foam, a 3D support grid and a self-inflating air layer. The kit includes a rechargeable USB-C pump, fitted microfiber sheet and carry case. Confirm pre-order timing before a trip.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_6: Snuuzu was listed at €899/$899 before any partner code. The higher price buys a different construction and integrated pump; it does not by itself prove better sleep for every person.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_7: Horizon was listed at $339 on sale when checked. Compare the final delivered price, warranty, returns and dispatch date rather than assuming the lower price always means better value.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_8: Snuuzu says its integrated rechargeable USB-C pump inflates the system in under two minutes. Treat this as a seller claim and charge the pump before travel.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_9: TESMAT supplies a separate rechargeable pump and claims inflation in under one minute; the self-inflating process can take about 20 minutes. Keep the pump with the packed kit.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_10: No universal winner is supported by the published data alone. Snuuzu emphasizes depth and an integrated pump; Horizon emphasizes a lower price and bundled kit.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.h3_10: Is Snuuzu worth the extra money over TESMAT?
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_11: Compare the specific features you will use, delivered price, warranty and return window. Trip frequency alone cannot prove that either mattress is worth its price.

vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.h3_12: Which is better for side sleepers?
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_13: We have not run a controlled side-sleeper pressure test. Thickness and construction are relevant, but body weight, firmness preference and vehicle setup also matter.
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.h3_13: Do both fit in the Model Y sub-trunk?
vs_snuuzu_vs_tesmat.p_14: Do not assume either bag fits every sub-trunk. Snuuzu publishes a 74 × 32 cm packed size; compare it with your vehicle and TESMAT’s current packed measurements before ordering.

vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.meta_description: Snuuzu vs Havnby FlatCore compared by price, size, slope support, pump, storage, trial and Model Y fit. Current facts checked July 2026.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.og_title: Snuuzu vs Havnby FlatCore: The Evidence-Based Comparison
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.og_description: Compare the current $899 Snuuzu and $599 Havnby FlatCore using published specifications, fit details and honest tradeoffs.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.h1: Snuuzu vs Havnby: Which Tesla Mattress Fits Your Trips?
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.hero_lede: Snuuzu prioritizes a longer layered sleep surface and rechargeable pump. Havnby FlatCore costs less and uses a deeper shaped profile with 12V setup. This guide separates measurable differences from comfort claims.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.hero_alt: Snuuzu Model Y mattress in an official studio product photograph
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.hero_caption: NovaPads manufacturer product image used for real Tesla-mattress context; it does not imply a hands-on test.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.verdict_title: Snuuzu leads on premium integration. Havnby makes the stronger price case.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.verdict_text: Choose Snuuzu when its 204 cm surface, rechargeable USB-C pump, washable Lyocell cover and integrated storage justify the $899 or €899 price. Choose Havnby FlatCore when a $599 Model Y-specific support profile, 12V pump and 60-day sleep trial better match the budget. Published specifications cannot identify one comfort winner for every sleeper.

vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.body_html:
Snuuzu vs Havnby specifications

Both products combine foam and air, but the similarity ends there. Snuuzu uses a layered comfort system over an adjustable surface-smoothing air section. FlatCore uses a shaped profile that is thickest where the folded Model Y seats need support. The figures below come from the current manufacturer pages and were checked on 23 July 2026.

FactorSnuuzu Model YHavnby FlatCore
List price€899 / $899$599
10% code example€809.10 / $809.10 with KLEPPE if eligible$539.10 with AWD if eligible
Vehicle fitAll Model Y versions, including JuniperModel Y 2020–2024 or 2025–2026 selection
Sleeping surface204 × 130 cm193 × 132 cm
Depth20 cm for Model Y19 cm maximum / 7.9 cm minimum
Listed weight10.6 kg package weight5.08 kg
Packed size74 × 32 cm67 × 43 cm
PumpRechargeable integrated USB-C pumpBuilt-in 12V ABS pump
Seller setup claimUnder 2 minutes80 sec inflate / 90 sec deflate
Warranty and trial3-year warranty3-year warranty and 60-day sleep trial

The $300 price gap changes the decision

At current list prices, Havnby costs $300 less than the US Snuuzu. If both 10% codes apply, the numerical gap remains $270. That is large enough to make “best value” a different question from “most complete premium system.” The code result, shipping, tax and regional currency still need to be confirmed in the live cart.

Snuuzu uses more of the budget on a long sleep surface, washable Lyocell cover, integrated storage design, rechargeable pump and replaceable-part promise. Havnby counters with lower entry cost, a published trial and a product that weighs less on the seller specification sheet. A frequent camper may value Snuuzu’s all-in-one packing method. An occasional camper may find FlatCore easier to justify.

Price check: compare the delivered total, not only the button price. Snuuzu has separate Europe and USA/Canada stores. Havnby uses model-year selections and may show different shipping or tax by destination.

Leveling approach and remaining headroom

A folded Model Y cargo floor slopes and changes height. Both products address that geometry, but they do it differently. Snuuzu places an adjustable air layer below the comfort foam. Havnby uses a contoured shape that reaches 19 cm at its thickest point and 7.9 cm at its thinnest.

The deeper end of either mattress brings the sleeper closer to the roof and hatch glass. That matters for sitting up, changing clothes and moving from side to side. Havnby’s level-surface claim should not be confused with automatically leveling the whole vehicle on uneven ground. Snuuzu also smooths the cargo-floor transition, but neither product replaces safe parking on a reasonably level site.

Pump power: rechargeable USB-C or the car’s 12V outlet

Snuuzu’s removable rechargeable pump keeps the setup independent of a trailing 12V cable. The tradeoff is battery management. The pump should be charged before leaving, and the USB-C cable belongs in the trip kit. The European product page currently states that newer packages include a spare rechargeable pump; confirm the contents in the selected region before ordering.

FlatCore uses a built-in 12V pump. It removes the separate charging routine, but setup depends on reaching the correct vehicle outlet and routing the cable without pinching it. Havnby quotes 80 seconds for inflation and 90 seconds for deflation. Snuuzu quotes under two minutes. These are seller claims, not timings recorded by TeslaMattress.

Couples, tall sleepers and storage

Snuuzu publishes 11 cm more length, while Havnby publishes 2 cm more width. Neither number guarantees usable body room because the hatch curve, pillow position and front-seat placement remove space. Tall sleepers should test their actual parked layout and close the hatch during the measurement. Couples should compare shoulder room at the narrowest part of the car rather than treating either product as a domestic double bed.

The packed shapes also differ. Snuuzu lists 74 × 32 cm and says the integrated bag fits Tesla storage areas. Havnby lists 67 × 43 cm. A longer, narrower roll may fit one compartment better than a shorter, wider package. Measure the intended storage space and include the sheet, pillows, charging cable and privacy screens.

Warranty, trial and opened-product risk

Both manufacturers advertise three-year warranties. Havnby also advertises a 60-day sleep trial. The current Snuuzu terms restrict used or unsealed mattress returns for hygiene reasons, and return shipping is generally paid by the customer unless the seller made the error. Read the exact regional policy before opening either product because a headline trial does not explain every eligibility condition.

Document the delivered condition, keep the packaging and test vehicle fit promptly. Warranty coverage addresses qualifying defects; it is not the same as a comfort guarantee.

Which should you buy?

Choose Snuuzu when

  • The 204 cm published surface matters for your measured sleeping position.
  • You prefer a rechargeable USB-C pump and integrated packing system.
  • A washable Lyocell cover and replaceable components justify the premium.
  • You camp often enough to value a more complete sleep system.

Choose Havnby FlatCore when

  • The $599 list price is a better match for the budget.
  • You want a Model Y-specific shaped support profile.
  • A built-in 12V pump suits how you set up camp.
  • The published 60-day trial is important after reading the conditions.
Evidence checked: current Snuuzu Model Y listing, Snuuzu US listing and Havnby FlatCore listing on 23 July 2026. We have not performed a controlled comfort or durability test.

vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_q1: Is Snuuzu worth the extra money over Havnby?
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_a1: Snuuzu can justify the higher price for buyers who value its 204 cm surface, rechargeable USB-C pump, washable Lyocell cover and integrated packing system. Havnby FlatCore has the stronger price case at $599 and adds a published 60-day sleep trial.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_q2: Which is flatter, Snuuzu or Havnby FlatCore?
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_a2: Both products are designed to reduce the folded-seat slope, but their methods differ. Snuuzu uses an adjustable surface-smoothing air layer. Havnby uses a shaped profile that reaches 19 cm at its thickest point. Neither product levels the entire car on uneven ground.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_q3: Which is better for tall Tesla Model Y sleepers?
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_a3: Snuuzu publishes a 204 cm surface and Havnby publishes 193 cm. Usable length also depends on pillow position, hatch shape and front-seat placement, so tall sleepers should measure the complete parked setup.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_q4: Do Snuuzu and Havnby both fit the Model Y Juniper?
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_a4: Both current sellers provide Juniper-compatible Model Y options. Snuuzu states that its Model Y mattress fits all Model Y versions. Havnby separates 2020–2024 and 2025–2026 choices, so select the exact year range.
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_q5: What are the current Snuuzu and Havnby discount codes?
vs_snuuzu_vs_havnby.faq_a5: TeslaMattress records code KLEPPE for 10% off eligible Snuuzu orders and code AWD for 10% off eligible Havnby orders. Code acceptance and stacking must be confirmed in the live cart.

vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.jsonld_1_name_1: Should I get Havnby FlatCore or CloudCore?
vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.jsonld_1_text_2: Choose FlatCore for a deeper Model Y-specific leveling profile. Choose CloudCore for lower weight, a lower $399 price and selectable Model S, 3, X and Y variants.
vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.jsonld_1_name_3: What's the difference between Havnby FlatCore and CloudCore?
vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.jsonld_1_text_4: FlatCore is a Model Y mattress with a shaped 7.9–19 cm profile and 5.08 kg listed weight. CloudCore has a published 11.5 cm profile, weighs 3.95 kg and is offered for Model S, 3, X and Y.

vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.p_3: Choose FlatCore for deeper, Model Y-specific leveling support. Choose CloudCore for broader Tesla model coverage, lower listed weight and a $200 lower observed price.

vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.h3_7: Is FlatCore worth $200 more than CloudCore?
vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.p_5: The premium is easier to justify when Model Y slope correction and maximum depth matter most. CloudCore is the stronger value when lower weight, a lower profile or another Tesla model matters more.
vs_havnby_autolevel_vs_foam.h3_8: Which is easier to set up?

vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_1_name_1: Can I use a Model Y mattress in a Model 3?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_1_text_2: Usually not. The vehicles have different cargo geometry and most current fitted mattresses use separate Model Y and Model 3 variants. Buy only the exact version the manufacturer lists for your model and year.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_1_name_3: What dimensions matter for a Model Y or Model 3 mattress?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_1_text_4: Use the mattress maker’s current sleeping-surface dimensions and fit diagram, then measure the narrowest width, usable length, seat transition and packed-storage area in your exact vehicle.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_1_name_5: Which Tesla is better for car camping?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_1_text_6: Model Y hatch access may simplify loading, while Model 3 can still support camping with an exact-fit setup. The better vehicle depends on occupants, body size, gear and the verified mattress geometry.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_3_description_1: Guide to mattress compatibility between Tesla Model Y and Model 3 vehicles
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_3_name_2: Tesla Model Y vs Model 3 Mattress Compatibility
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_3_description_3: Mattresses designed for Tesla Model Y cargo area
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.jsonld_3_description_5: Mattresses designed for Tesla Model 3 cargo area
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h1: Model Y vs Model 3: Tesla Mattress Compatibility Guide
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p: Everything you need to know about sizing, fit, and which mattresses work with which Tesla.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h2: Quick Answer: Can I Use the Same Mattress?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p_2: Usually not. Current fitted systems typically have separate Model Y and Model 3 variants. Similar branding does not make the shapes interchangeable; CloudCore, for example, still requires the correct Model 3 or Model Y year selection.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h2_3: How the Vehicle Layout Changes Setup
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p_3: Model Y has hatch access and a different cargo profile; Model 3 uses a sedan trunk opening and different seat-to-cargo geometry. Neither layout proves that a given mattress fits.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h3_3: Can I use a Model Y mattress in a Model 3?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p_6: Usually not. Buy the exact current variant listed for your Tesla model and year; do not infer compatibility from similar width or product branding.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h3_4: Which Tesla is better for car camping?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p_7: Model Y hatch access can simplify loading, while Model 3 remains viable with the right fitted product. Occupants, gear and verified fit matter more than a blanket winner.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h3_5: How do I know whether the usable length is enough?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p_8: Use the product’s published sleeping length and measure your sleeping position with the front seats set as intended. Do not rely on a universal height cutoff.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h3_6: Is there a mattress that fits both Model Y and Model 3?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p_9: Some brands sell the same product family in separate Model Y and Model 3 variants. That is not the same as one mattress fitting both; select the exact SKU.
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.h3_7: Do 2024/2025 refresh models use the same mattresses?
vs_model_y_vs_model_3.p_10: Do not assume refresh compatibility. Check the current manufacturer year range and fit notes for Highland, Juniper or any later update before ordering.

How to use the code

  1. Open the correct regional store: Europe or USA and Canada.
  2. Select the exact product, Tesla model and regional store you need.
  3. Add the item to the cart and enter KLEPPE in the discount field.
  4. Check that the subtotal changes before completing payment.

If nothing changes, do not assume the saving will be added later. Recheck spelling, product eligibility, currency and any automatic sale already applied. Store support—not TeslaMattress—controls code acceptance and refunds.

How much could it save?

On an eligible $100 subtotal, 10% would equal $10 before tax and shipping. Apply the code to your actual cart rather than treating this example as a promised total. A larger advertised percentage is not automatically the cheapest deal: compare the final delivered price, included accessories, warranty and return conditions.

What this page does not promise

Codes can be limited by product, region, customer type or promotion. Unless the live store explicitly says otherwise, do not assume the code stacks with sale pricing, has no minimum purchase or has no expiry. Shipping speed, tax, duties and return eligibility also depend on the merchant and destination.

Skip the code if…

  • An automatic store promotion produces a lower final total.
  • The code requires a product or bundle you did not intend to buy.
  • The delivered price is worse than a suitable alternative.
  • The live warranty or return terms do not meet your needs.

Our checking standard

We keep the last recorded checkout date visible and do not silently move it forward during a copy edit. The live cart remains the final authority. Read our research methodology and affiliate disclosure for how partner links and evidence are handled.

guide_best_mattresses.og_title: Tesla Camping Mattress Buying Guide: 11 Options Compared
